design_analysis.overview: Guidance for analyzing and implementing Carbon components from images, screenshots, and user descriptions without Figma MCP access.

design_scale_analysis.title: Design Scale Analysis (MANDATORY for Images)
design_scale_analysis.description: Understanding the scale and dimensions of the original design is a CRITICAL first step that significantly impacts implementation accuracy. Design scale analysis MUST ALWAYS be performed for any images provided, and the design scale MUST ALWAYS be applied to any sizes measured in the image.

requirement.title: AUTOMATICALLY perform design scale analysis
requirement.description: Design scale analysis MUST be performed AUTOMATICALLY for ANY images provided. This is NOT optional and MUST be done immediately without waiting for user prompting.

requirement.title: Always apply design scale to measurements
requirement.description: The calculated design scale MUST be applied to ALL sizes measured in any image. This ensures accurate implementation of typography, spacing, and component dimensions.

requirement.title: Use ONLY the mandatory question format
requirement.description:
When asking about image dimensions, you MUST ONLY use this EXACT format: "The image you have provided is [width]px x [height]px. Which of the following describes the image best?"
With EXACTLY these three options: 1. "The image is the actual size of our UI." 2. "The image is smaller, scale up so that [dimension] is [larger]px." 3. "The image is larger, scale down so that [dimension] is [smaller]px."

step.title: Determine design dimensions
step.action: Identify the dimensions of provided images and ask for scale using ONLY the mandatory question format
step.mandatory_example: <ask_followup_question> <question>The image you have provided is 1920px x 1080px. Which of the following describes the image best?</question> <follow_up> <suggest>The image is the actual size of our UI.</suggest> <suggest>The image is smaller, scale up so that the width is 1920px.</suggest> <suggest>The image is larger, scale down so that the width is 1280px.</suggest> </follow_up> </ask_followup_question>

step.title: Calculate scale factor
step.action: Calculate the scale factor based on user's response
step.formula: Scale Factor = Target Size / Current Size

example.result: All measurements should be multiplied by 0.667
All measurements should be multiplied by 1.6

step.title: Apply scale to all measurements
step.action: Apply the calculated scale factor to ALL measurements from the image
step.critical_rule: EVERY measurement taken from the image MUST be multiplied by the scale factor before mapping to Carbon tokens or using in implementation.

carbon_reference_sizes.title: Carbon Component and Token Reference
carbon_reference_sizes.description: Use these reference sizes when analyzing scaled designs to map to Carbon tokens.

image_analysis.title: Complete Image Analysis Workflow
image_analysis.description: After establishing design scale, perform complete analysis to identify Carbon components, layout structure, and implementation approach.

step.title: Component Identification
step.action: Identify all UI elements and map to Carbon components

guidance.point: Look for standard patterns (buttons, inputs, tables, cards, tiles)
Identify component variants (primary/secondary buttons, clickable tiles, etc.)
Note any custom components that need to be built with Carbon primitives
Use Carbon MCP to verify component availability and correct package

step.title: Layout Analysis
step.action: Analyze layout structure and grid requirements

guidance.point: Identify logical content groups (header, content sections, footer)
Each logical group needs its own Grid component
Estimate column spans based on visual proportions
Note responsive behavior requirements

estimation_guidelines.title: Column Span Estimation
estimation_guidelines.guideline: Third-width elements → sm={4} md={4} lg={5} or lg={6}
Tiles that wrap → Each tile gets equal columns that add up to 16 at lg

step.title: Typography and Spacing
step.action: Identify typography hierarchy and spacing patterns

guidance.point: Map headings to Carbon type tokens (heading-01 through heading-07)
Identify body text styles (body-short-01, body-short-02, body-long-01, body-long-02)
Measure visual spacing in the design, not element bounds
Estimate spacing using Carbon spacing scale (8px increments)
Use Carbon MCP to look up exact token values if needed

typography_mapping.title: Common Typography Patterns
typography_mapping.pattern: Large page titles → heading-04 or heading-05

spacing_guidelines.guideline: Use the closest Carbon spacing tokens when other layout criteria (such as Carbon Grid) do not apply
Be cautious with margins as they collapse in some circumstances. Consider using padding or gap properties when consistent spacing is critical.
Spacing should match the visual layout of the design and not be affected by non-visible artifacts that may overflow the visual size of an element.

guidance.point: Determine which Carbon theme is being used (white, g10, g90, g100)
Identify layer usage: When elements have background colors matching layer-aware values ($layer-01, $layer-02, $layer-03), evaluate if they should be implemented as Layer components
Note any custom colors that need theme tokens
Look for elevated surfaces that might need Layer component

theme_identification.theme: Light theme with subtle gray background
Dark theme with dark gray background

layer_detection.title: Detecting Layers in Images
layer_detection.description: Images often don't explicitly indicate Carbon's layer system. Look for these indicators:

indicators.indicator: Background color changes that match Carbon layer token values
Nested containers with distinct background colors
Cards, panels, or sections elevated above the base background
Modal dialogs or overlays (typically require Layer)
Form sections or grouped content with different backgrounds

evaluation_criteria.criterion: Does the element have a background color matching $layer-01, $layer-02, or $layer-03?
Is the element a container for other components that need proper theming?
Would nested Carbon components benefit from automatic layer context?
Does the design show visual hierarchy through background color changes?

implementation_decision.rule: If an element has a background matching a layer token value AND contains Carbon components, implement it as a Layer component
If an element is purely decorative with a layer-like color but contains no Carbon components, use custom styling instead
When in doubt, prefer Layer components for containers with Carbon components inside

guideline.title: Rely on Nesting and Context
guideline.description: NEVER manually set the layer level using the level prop. The Layer component automatically manages layer levels through nesting and context.
guideline.correct_usage: // CORRECT: Let nesting determine layer level <> <ComponentOnBaseLayer /> <Layer> <ComponentOnLayer01 /> <Layer> <ComponentOnLayer02 /> </Layer> </Layer> </>

guideline.title: Use withBackground for Visible Layers
guideline.description: The Layer component does NOT set its own background color by default. When an image shows a visible background color change, use the withBackground prop to apply the background color.
guideline.correct_usage:
// CORRECT: Use withBackground when design shows visible background

Card Title

Card content with proper layer context

step.title: Present Analysis to User
step.action: Share findings with user before implementation

required_sections.section: Component Inventory - List all Carbon components needed with variants
Layout Structure - Grid configuration with column spans for all breakpoints
Typography - Type token mapping for all text elements
Spacing - Estimated spacing tokens between elements
Color/Theme - Theme identification and layer usage

step.critical_rule: ALWAYS pause after presenting the analysis and wait for user confirmation before proceeding with implementation.
step.example_presentation:
Based on the image provided, here's my analysis:
## Component Inventory - Button (primary) - for main action - Button (secondary) - for cancel action - TextInput - for email field - PasswordInput - for password field - Tile - for feature cards (3 tiles) - Grid, Column - for layout
## Layout Structure 1. Header Section (separate Grid): - Full width: sm={4} md={8} lg={16}
2. Form Section (separate Grid): - Form container: sm={4} md={6} lg={8} (centered)
3. Features Section (separate Grid): - 3 tiles: sm={4} md={4} lg={5}, lg={5}, lg={6}
## Typography - Page title: heading-04 - Section headings: heading-03 - Tile titles: heading-02 - Body text: body-long-01 - Form labels: label-01
## Spacing - Between sections: $spacing-09 (48px) - Between form fields: $spacing-05 (16px) - Tile internal padding: $spacing-05 (16px)
## Theme - White theme (light background) - No layer changes detected
Would you like me to proceed with this implementation approach?

limitations.limitation: May need user clarification on specific details
Cannot access design system variables or tokens directly

workarounds.workaround: Use Carbon MCP to research component APIs and examples
Estimate using Carbon spacing scale (8px increments)
Ask user for clarification when details are ambiguous
Use standard Carbon patterns and conventions

structured_assessment_format.title: Structured Assessment Output Format
structured_assessment_format.description: When presenting design analysis, use this structured format to ensure comprehensive coverage of all implementation aspects. This format matches the expected assessment structure for thorough design documentation.

section.name: components
section.title: Components
section.content: List all Carbon components with specific variants
section.example: ## Components - Button (primary, secondary) - Tile (clickable variant) - DataTable with pagination - TextInput with validation - Dropdown (multi-select)

section.name: icons
section.title: Icons
section.content: List all icons with their sizes
section.example: ## Icons - Add icon (16px) - Edit icon (16px) - Delete icon (16px) - ArrowRight icon (20px)

section.name: layout
section.title: Layout
section.content: Describe the visual arrangement and structure
section.example: ## Layout The page uses a two-column layout with a sidebar on the left (4 columns) and main content area on the right (12 columns). The main content area contains a header section followed by a grid of tiles arranged in 4 columns.

section.name: grid_layout
section.title: Grid Layout
section.content: Specify column spans for ALL breakpoints (sm, md, lg)

section.name: spacing
section.title: Spacing
section.content: Document all spacing with Carbon token mappings
section.example: ## Spacing - Gap between header and content: 24px → $spacing-06 - Card internal padding: 16px → $spacing-05 - Gap between tiles: 16px → $spacing-05 (using gap property) - Section margins: 32px → $spacing-07 - Button spacing: 8px → $spacing-03

section.name: background
section.title: Background
section.example: ## Background - Page background: $background - Header section: $layer with subtle gradient to $layer-accent - Tiles: $layer-01 (using Layer component) - Sidebar: $layer-02 (nested Layer component)

section.name: other_details
section.title: Other Details
section.example: ## Other Details - Typography: Page title uses heading-05, section headings use heading-03, body text uses body-long-01 - Grid variant: narrow grid (16px gaps) for tile section - Accessibility: All interactive elements have proper ARIA labels - Responsive behavior: Tiles stack on mobile, 2 columns on tablet, 4 columns on desktop - Theme: Uses white theme (g10) with proper token usage for theme compatibility

assessment_template.title: Complete Assessment Template
assessment_template.template:
# Design Assessment: [Component/Page Name]
## Components [List all Carbon components with variants]
## Icons [List all icons with sizes]
## Layout [Describe visual arrangement]
## Grid Layout [Specify column spans for sm, md, lg breakpoints for ALL sections]
### [Section Name] (separate Grid) - [Element]: sm={X} md={Y} lg={Z}
### [Nested Section] (nested Grid within parent Column) - [Element]: sm={X} md={Y} lg={Z}
## Spacing [Document all spacing with token mappings] - [Location]: [pixels]px → [token]
## Background [Describe background treatment] - [Element]: [token or gradient description]
## Other Details - Typography: [mappings to Carbon type tokens] - Grid variant: [default/narrow/condensed with rationale] - Accessibility: [considerations] - Responsive behavior: [description] - Theme: [theme identification] - [Any other relevant implementation notes]

critical_requirements.requirement: ALL sections must be included in every assessment
Grid layout MUST specify column spans for sm, md, and lg breakpoints
Spacing MUST include both pixel values and Carbon token mappings
Clearly distinguish between separate Grids and nested Grids

description_based_implementation.title: Implementing from User Descriptions
description_based_implementation.description: When users describe desired functionality without providing designs, guide them through requirements gathering and propose Carbon-based solutions.

steps.step: Clarify the component's purpose and user interactions

example_questions.question: What is the primary purpose of this component?
What actions should users be able to perform?
What data needs to be displayed or collected?
Should it work on mobile devices?
Are there any specific accessibility requirements?

component_recommendation.title: Recommending Carbon Components

guidance.point: Use Carbon MCP to search for similar patterns and examples
Recommend official Carbon components over custom builds
Propose composition of multiple components when needed
Present options with pros/cons when multiple approaches exist
Explain how Carbon components meet the requirements

component_recommendation.example:
User: "I need a login form"
Analysis and Proposal:
## Component Inventory - TextInput (for username/email) - PasswordInput (for password) - Button (primary for submit) - Link (for "Forgot password") - Checkbox (optional "Remember me") - Form (wrapper)
## Layout Proposal - Single column form centered on page - Form container: sm={4} md={6} lg={8} - Stack inputs vertically with $spacing-05 between
## Implementation Approach 1. Use Form component as wrapper 2. TextInput with proper labelText and validation 3. PasswordInput with show/hide toggle 4. Button with type="submit" 5. Link for password recovery
## Accessibility Features - Proper labels for all inputs (labelText prop) - Error states with clear messages (invalid, invalidText props) - Keyboard navigation support (built into Carbon components) - Screen reader announcements (ARIA attributes included)
Would you like me to proceed with this approach, or would you like to adjust anything?

iterative_refinement.title: Iterative Refinement
iterative_refinement.description: Work with the user to refine the implementation based on their feedback.

approach.step: Present initial proposal with component choices
Get user feedback on the approach

design_fidelity_without_figma.title: Design Fidelity Guidelines
design_fidelity_without_figma.description: When working from images or descriptions, aim for Carbon-compliant implementations that match the intent rather than pixel-perfect recreation.

principle.title: Use Standard Carbon Components and Patterns
principle.description: Leverage Carbon's built-in components rather than creating custom solutions. This ensures consistency, accessibility, and maintainability.

principle.title: Follow Carbon Spacing Scale
principle.description: Use Carbon spacing tokens (8px increments) rather than arbitrary values. This maintains visual rhythm and consistency.

principle.title: Use Carbon Type Tokens
principle.description: Map typography to Carbon type tokens rather than custom font sizes. This ensures proper hierarchy and readability.

principle.title: Clarify Ambiguous Details
principle.description: When details are unclear from images or descriptions, ask the user for clarification rather than making assumptions.

when_to_ask_questions.title: When to Ask for Clarification

scenarios.scenario: Ambiguous component variants (e.g., which button kind to use)
Uncertain about data structure or content
Specific interaction patterns not shown in image

when_to_ask_questions.example: <ask_followup_question> <question>I see three cards in the design. Should these be clickable tiles that navigate somewhere, or static tiles for displaying information?</question> <follow_up> <suggest>Clickable tiles that navigate to detail pages</suggest> <suggest>Static tiles for displaying information only</suggest> <suggest>Expandable tiles that show more content when clicked</suggest> </follow_up> </ask_followup_question>

common_patterns.title: Common Implementation Patterns
common_patterns.description: Frequently used patterns when implementing from images or descriptions.

layout.grid: Separate Grids for header and tiles

pattern.name: Modal Dialog
pattern.note: Modals don't use Grid - they have their own layout system

carbon_mcp_integration.title: Using Carbon MCP for Design Analysis
carbon_mcp_integration.description: Leverage Carbon MCP to enhance design analysis and implementation.

step.action: Search for similar components or patterns

step.description: Use code examples from Carbon MCP to understand correct prop usage
